title: On certain complex surface singularities
abstract: The thesis deals with holomorphic germs $ Φ: (\mathbb{C}^2, 0) \to (\mathbb{C}^3,0) $ singular only at the origin, with a special emphasis on the distinguished class of finitely determined germs. The results are published in two articles (arXiv:1404.2853 and arXiv:1902.01229), joint with András Némethi. In Chapter 3 of the thesis we study the associated immersion $ S^3 \looparrowright S^5 $, while Chapter 5 contains an algorithm providing the Milnor fibre boundary of the non-isolated hypersurface singularity determined by the image of $ Φ$. These results create bridges between different areas of complex singularity theory and immersion theory. The background of these topics is summerized in Chapter 1, 2 and 4.
comment: Eötvös Loránd University PhD thesis. Contains mostly the results of arXiv:1404.2853 and arXiv:1902.01229. Adviser: Prof. András Némethi
